cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer. The question is asking which of the options is NOT a reason why the English language spread to various parts of the world. The passage provided gives some information about this. Looking at the passage, the first sentence says that the English language was introduced to the Americas by British colonization, starting in 1607. Then it mentions that the language spread to other parts due to British trade and colonization elsewhere, as well as the spread of the former British Empire. The part about the British Empire having a population of 470-570 million under its control by 1921 is a statistic to show the extent of their influence. So the reasons given in the text for the spread of English are British colonization, British trade, and the expansion of the British Empire. The options are (A) British colonization, (B) the spread of the former English Empire, (C) British trade around the world, and (D) a large British population. The passage doesn't mention a large British population as a reason for the spread of English.
